perturbation.original_text: 5. Any Proprietary Information and copies thereof disclosed by either Party to the other shall remain the property of the dis closing Party and shall be returned by the receiving Party immediately upon request. The above provisions shall not apply to the Proprietary Information disclosed by the Supplier to the Buyer and which fall und er clause 12.2 of the General Purchase Conditions.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Any Data and copies thereof disclosed by either Party to the other shall remain the property of the dis closing Party and shall be returned by the receiving Party immediately upon request. The above provisions shall not apply to the Proprietary Information disclosed by the Supplier to the Buyer and which fall und er clause 12.2 of the General Purchase Conditions.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Proprietary Information' is replaced with 'Data' at the beginning of Section 5. This creates confusion because the first part of the paragraph refers to 'Data' needing to be returned, while the exception at the end refers to 'Proprietary Information' under clause 12.2. It's unclear whether clause 12.2 now applies only to 'Proprietary Information' that is not considered 'Data', which creates ambiguity. It also contradicts the section above that defines Proprietary Information, as Data is not defined in a similar fashion, so there is ambiguity on which data are to be returned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any Data and copies thereof disclosed by either Party to the other shall remain the property of the dis closing Party and shall be returned by the receiving Party immediately upon request. The above provisions shall not apply to the Proprietary Information disclosed by the Supplier to the Buyer and which fall und er clause 12.2 of the General Purchase Conditions.
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Data' in Section 5, while the exception still refers to 'Proprietary Information' under clause 12.2. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, however the explanation states that this change causes ambiguity regarding whether clause 12.2 applies only to 'Proprietary Information' that is not considered 'Data'. This creates a direct conflict and contradiction because it is unclear which type of information is governed by which clause, aligning with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, which states that contradictions occur when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Except as provided under clause 12.2 of the General Purchase Conditions, it is expressly understood and agreed by the Par ties hereto that the disclosure and provision of Proprietary Information under this Agreement by a Party to the other Party shall notbe construed as granting to the receiving Party under the laws of any country any rights whether expressed or implied by licence or otherwise on the matters, inventions or discoveries to which such Proprietary Information pertains or any copyright, trademar k or trade secret rights. The property in all information and/or data disclosed by the Buyer to the Supplier pursuant to this Agreement shall subject t o any right of any other owner, rest with the Buyer.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Except as provided under clause 12.2 of the General Purchase Conditions, it is expressly understood and agreed by the Par ties hereto that the disclosure and provision of Confidential Information under this Agreement by a Party to the other Party shall notbe construed as granting to the receiving Party under the laws of any country any rights whether expressed or implied by licence or otherwise on the matters, inventions or discoveries to which such Proprietary Information pertains or any copyright, trademar k or trade secret rights. The property in all information and/or data disclosed by the Buyer to the Supplier pursuant to this Agreement shall subject t o any right of any other owner, rest with the Buyer.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Proprietary Information' is replaced with 'Confidential Information' in the first part of Section 8, while the clause still uses 'Proprietary Information' in reference to 'the matters, inventions or discoveries to which such Proprietary Information pertains'. This contradicts the definition, because it creates the risk of having Confidential Information, not consider Proprietary, as the agreement is reffering, leading to uncertainty about what rights are being granted or withheld. Is the agreement withholding granting rights to Confidential Information or Proprietary?
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as provided under clause 12.2 of the General Purchase Conditions, it is expressly understood and agreed by the Par ties hereto that the disclosure and provision of Confidential Information under this Agreement by a Party to the other Party shall notbe construed as granting to the receiving Party under the laws of any country any rights whether expressed or implied by licence or otherwise on the matters, inventions or discoveries to which such Proprietary Information pertains or any copyright, trademar k or trade secret rights. The property in all information and/or data disclosed by the Buyer to the Supplier pursuant to this Agreement shall subject t o any right of any other owner, rest with the Buyer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Confidential Information' in the first part of Section 8, but retains 'Proprietary Information' in the latter part, creating a contradiction. The contradicted text mirrors this change. This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty about which type of information the clause is referring to, causing a direct conflict as the agreement now refers to both 'Confidential Information' and 'Proprietary Information' within the same clause without clarifying which one applies to 'matters, inventions or discoveries'. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 11. The end or termination of this Agreement shall not relieve the receiving Party of complying with the obligations imposed by paragraph 4 thereof with respect to the use and protection of the Proprietary Information received prior to the date of termi nation or end of this Agreement. Such obligations shall continue for the period applicable as set forth in said paragraph.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. The end or termination of this Agreement shall not relieve the receiving Party of complying with the obligations imposed by paragraph 4 thereof with respect to the use and protection of the Confidential Material received prior to the date of termi nation or end of this Agreement. Such obligations shall continue for the period applicable as set forth in said paragraph.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Proprietary Information' with 'Confidential Material' in Section 11 creates a conflict. Since paragraph 4 specifically refers to obligations regarding 'Proprietary Information', it's now unclear if those obligations still apply to 'Confidential Material' post-termination. The term 'Confidential Material' is not defined, leading to ambiguity and making the obligations uncertain, creating a situation, that what is not Proprietary Information, is it still Confidential Material or vice versa.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The end or termination of this Agreement shall not relieve the receiving Party of complying with the obligations imposed by paragraph 4 thereof with respect to the use and protection of the Confidential Material received prior to the date of termi nation or end of this Agreement. Such obligations shall continue for the period applicable as set forth in said paragraph.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Confidential Material' in Section 11. The explanation states that paragraph 4 specifically refers to obligations regarding 'Proprietary Information'. This creates ambiguity because it's now unclear if the obligations in paragraph 4, which refer to 'Proprietary Information', also apply to 'Confidential Material'. Because the terms are now used independently, but potentially referring to the same obligation with different nomenclature, it creates a contradiction. The contradiction text and change text are in the same section, so this demonstrates the confusion of having the Proprietary Information be 'Confidential Material'.
